title: Conversational RAG
constraints: The agent must maintain conversation history across turns
Follow-up questions must be resolved using prior context
The retriever must receive contextualized queries, not raw follow-ups
The agent must still retrieve documents for each turn
hints: Store conversation history and use it to reformulate follow-up questions into standalone queries
Before retrieval, combine the chat history with the current question to create a contextualized search query
Use a history-aware retriever or a question condensation step before the main retrieval

starterCode.langchain:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI, OpenAIEmbeddings
from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate
from langchain_community.vectorstores import FAISS
from langchain_core.documents import Document

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")
embeddings = OpenAIEmbeddings()

documents = [
    Document(page_content="ProductX is an enterprise CRM platform starting at $99/month."),
    Document(page_content="ProductY is a project management tool priced at $49/month per team."),
    Document(page_content="ProductX offers advanced analytics, custom dashboards, and API access."),
    Document(page_content="ProductY includes Gantt charts, sprint planning, and time tracking."),
    Document(page_content="ProductX has a 14-day free trial with full feature access."),
]

vectorstore = FAISS.from_documents(documents, embeddings)
retriever = vectorstore.as_retriever()

prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([
    ("system", "Answer based on context.\n\nContext: {context}"),
    ("human", "{question}"),
])

# BUG: Each query is treated independently — no conversation history
def ask(question: str) -> str:
    docs = retriever.invoke(question)
    context = "\n".join([doc.page_content for doc in docs])
    chain = prompt | llm
    result = chain.invoke({"context": context, "question": question})
    return result.content

print(ask("Tell me about ProductX."))
# Follow-up: "What about pricing?" — but the agent doesn't know we're still talking about ProductX
print(ask("What about pricing?"))
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from crewai import LLM
from crewai.tools import tool
from langchain_openai import OpenAIEmbeddings
from langchain_community.vectorstores import FAISS
from langchain_core.documents import Document

llm = LLM(model="gpt-4o-mini")
embeddings = OpenAIEmbeddings()

documents = [
    Document(page_content="ProductX is an enterprise CRM platform starting at $99/month."),
    Document(page_content="ProductY is a project management tool priced at $49/month per team."),
    Document(page_content="ProductX offers advanced analytics, custom dashboards, and API access."),
    Document(page_content="ProductY includes Gantt charts, sprint planning, and time tracking."),
    Document(page_content="ProductX has a 14-day free trial with full feature access."),
]

vectorstore = FAISS.from_documents(documents, embeddings)
retriever = vectorstore.as_retriever()

@tool
def search_products(query: str) -> str:
    """Search product documentation for relevant information."""
    docs = retriever.invoke(query)
    return "\n".join([doc.page_content for doc in docs])

agent = Agent(
    role="Product Q&A Specialist",
    goal="Answer questions about products",
    backstory="You are a product specialist. Answer based on retrieved context.",
    tools=[search_products],
    llm=llm,
)

# BUG: Each query is treated independently — no conversation history
task1 = Task(description="Tell me about ProductX.", expected_output="Product information", agent=agent)
crew1 = Crew(agents=[agent], tasks=[task1])
print(crew1.kickoff())

# Follow-up: "What about pricing?" — but the agent doesn't know we're still talking about ProductX
task2 = Task(description="What about pricing?", expected_output="Pricing information", agent=agent)
crew2 = Crew(agents=[agent], tasks=[task2])
print(crew2.kickoff())
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner
from agents.tool import function_tool
from langchain_openai import OpenAIEmbeddings
from langchain_community.vectorstores import FAISS
from langchain_core.documents import Document

embeddings = OpenAIEmbeddings()

documents = [
    Document(page_content="ProductX is an enterprise CRM platform starting at $99/month."),
    Document(page_content="ProductY is a project management tool priced at $49/month per team."),
    Document(page_content="ProductX offers advanced analytics, custom dashboards, and API access."),
    Document(page_content="ProductY includes Gantt charts, sprint planning, and time tracking."),
    Document(page_content="ProductX has a 14-day free trial with full feature access."),
]

vectorstore = FAISS.from_documents(documents, embeddings)
retriever = vectorstore.as_retriever()

@function_tool
def search_products(query: str) -> str:
    """Search product documentation for relevant information."""
    docs = retriever.invoke(query)
    return "\n".join([doc.page_content for doc in docs])

agent = Agent(
    name="Product Q&A",
    instructions="Answer questions about products. Use the search tool to find relevant information.",
    tools=[search_products],
)

# BUG: Each query is treated independently — no conversation history
print(Runner.run_sync(agent, "Tell me about ProductX.").final_output)

# Follow-up: "What about pricing?" — but the agent doesn't know we're still talking about ProductX
print(Runner.run_sync(agent, "What about pricing?").final_output)

evaluationCriteria.label: Maintains conversation history
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent tracks prior turns and uses them to understand follow-up questions

evaluationCriteria.label: Resolves follow-up references
evaluationCriteria.description: Ambiguous follow-ups like 'What about pricing?' are correctly resolved to the right product

evaluationCriteria.label: Retrieves with conversational context
evaluationCriteria.description: The retriever receives contextualized queries that incorporate prior conversation context
